cases.prompt: A user reports 'the export sometimes produces an empty file.' What is your very first step, before reading any code?
A Python service log shows a long stack trace ending in 'AttributeError: NoneType object has no attribute id', preceded by several other errors. How do you read this log before forming a theory?

A regression appeared sometime in the last 200 commits; the test suite can detect it in 30 seconds. What is the most efficient way to find the offending commit, and roughly how many test runs will it take?
You've read the suspect function for 15 minutes and still can't see the bug. The function transforms a list of records. What do you do next?
You have three ideas about what's causing a flaky test: a race condition, a stale cache, and a timezone bug. Describe your process for investigating.

checks.value: (?i)one (hypothesis|change|thing|idea) at a time|one at a time|single (hypothesis|change)

cases.prompt: You've edited the code four times but the error message never changes at all — it's as if your edits do nothing. What should you suspect and how do you check?
You've spent triple your estimate on a bug, killed three hypotheses, and the remaining lead requires production database access you don't have. What do you do, and what does your write-up contain?
